verses.text: God recalls 'the kindness of thy youth,' when Israel followed Him through the wilderness, holy to the LORD. He remembers their first love. The early devotion of His people is precious in His memory.
God recalls 'the kindness of thy youth,' when Israel followed Him through the wilderness, holy to the LORD. He remembers their first love. The early devotion of His people is precious in His memory.
God recalls 'the kindness of thy youth,' when Israel followed Him through the wilderness, holy to the LORD. He remembers their first love. The early devotion of His people is precious in His memory.

He asks what wrong they found in Him, that they went after vanity; even priests and prophets did not ask, 'Where is the LORD?' They forsook Him without cause. Apostasy is the more inexcusable when God has given no reason for it.

He asks what wrong they found in Him, that they went after vanity; even priests and prophets did not ask, 'Where is the LORD?' They forsook Him without cause. Apostasy is the more inexcusable when God has given no reason for it.

He asks what wrong they found in Him, that they went after vanity; even priests and prophets did not ask, 'Where is the LORD?' They forsook Him without cause. Apostasy is the more inexcusable when God has given no reason for it.
No nation changes its gods, though they are no gods; 'but my people have changed their glory for that which doth not profit.' The exchange is absurd. They traded the living God for worthless idols.
No nation changes its gods, though they are no gods; 'but my people have changed their glory for that which doth not profit.' The exchange is absurd. They traded the living God for worthless idols.
No nation changes its gods, though they are no gods; 'but my people have changed their glory for that which doth not profit.' The exchange is absurd. They traded the living God for worthless idols.

Let the heavens be astonished, for 'they have forsaken me the fountain of living waters, and hewed... cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.' Two evils are named. They left the spring of life for leaky pits that hold nothing.

Let the heavens be astonished, for 'they have forsaken me the fountain of living waters, and hewed... cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.' Two evils are named. They left the spring of life for leaky pits that hold nothing.
Israel, no born slave, has become a prey; the lions roar over him, and Egypt has broken his crown. Bondage results from his sin. Forsaking God leaves a people exposed to every enemy.
Israel, no born slave, has become a prey; the lions roar over him, and Egypt has broken his crown. Bondage results from his sin. Forsaking God leaves a people exposed to every enemy.
Israel, no born slave, has become a prey; the lions roar over him, and Egypt has broken his crown. Bondage results from his sin. Forsaking God leaves a people exposed to every enemy.
His own wickedness has brought this; 'know therefore and see that it is an evil thing and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the LORD.' Sin is self-punishing. To leave God is its own bitter chastening.
His own wickedness has brought this; 'know therefore and see that it is an evil thing and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the LORD.' Sin is self-punishing. To leave God is its own bitter chastening.
His own wickedness has brought this; 'know therefore and see that it is an evil thing and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the LORD.' Sin is self-punishing. To leave God is its own bitter chastening.

Of old He broke their yoke, yet they played the harlot under every green tree; their guilt is marked before Him though they wash with soap. Sin's stain is deep. No outward scrubbing removes the guilt God sees within.

Of old He broke their yoke, yet they played the harlot under every green tree; their guilt is marked before Him though they wash with soap. Sin's stain is deep. No outward scrubbing removes the guilt God sees within.

They deny defilement, yet run after idols like 'a swift dromedary' in heat; they say, 'There is no hope,' loving strangers. Their passion for sin is shameless. The heart enslaved to idols cannot be reasoned from its course.

As a caught thief is ashamed, so is Israel, who calls a stock 'my father'; yet in trouble they cry, 'Arise, and save us.' Their idols cannot help. Men turn to false gods in ease and to the true God only in distress.

Why plead with God, when He has corrected them in vain? 'Can a maid forget her ornaments?... yet my people have forgotten me days without number.' Their forgetfulness astounds. They prize trinkets more than the God who made them.

Skilled in seeking lovers, with 'the blood of the poor innocents' on their skirts, they shall be ashamed of Egypt as of Assyria. Their alliances will fail. Those who trust the nations instead of God reap only disappointment.
